75901.subject: Israel’s Palantir Rival Is Selling $1 Million Vans To Help U.S. Cops Snoop
75901.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/israels-palantir-rival-selling-1-million-vans-help-us-8chyc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BOTtNbWigTheG6%2FaB%2B4FyZA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1hAar_ufJBIck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrjq86tp~4y-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrjq86tp-4y
  
Good evening, Israeli surveillance company Cognyte, which touts itself as the “leading alternative” to $315 billion market cap Palantir, made most of its 2025 revenue in Israel and Europe. But it’s slowly making inroads in the U.S. by selling surveillance tech—including software that claims to find leads in big police datasets and predict where crime is likely to occur in the future. Here’s what to know. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads NurPhoto via Getty Images AI Model Wars: Anthropic Extends Fable Access Again After OpenAI’s Sol Release OpenAI claimed Sol outperformed Anthropic’s Fable in coding tasks and that its rival’s model failed to test for questions involving scientific research. Jose Luis Magana via Associated Press As Investment Fraud Losses Hit $8.6 Billion, The SEC Rethinks A Job It Was Already Supposed To Be Doing A new SEC working group devoted to the fraud problem is reassuring, though it also raises an obvious question about what the agency wasn’t already doing.
Приєднатися до обговорення
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/israels-palantir-rival-selling-1-million-vans-help-us-8chyc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BOTtNbWigTheG6%2FaB%2B4FyZA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1hAar_ufJBIck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-fz96fz~mrjq86tp~4y-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrjq86tp-4y

75894.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Trump presses Senate on the Clarity Act
75894.body:
Strategy sells $467 million in MSTR shares, makes no bitcoin purchases as USD reserve hits $3 billion | Bitcoin whale moves $188 million in BTC after 7-year dormancy | SBI Holdings, Solana Foundation partner to build onchain financial market | Robinhood Chain draws over $3 billion in weekly DEX volume to join top five chains |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Daniel Kuhn

Happy Monday! *Bitcoin* slipped back below $63,000 after a weekend of renewed U.S.-Iran strikes sapped risk appetite, while *ether* pared an early month-high open to hover near $1,770.

In today's newsletter, *President Trump* makes a renewed push for the *Clarity Act* , *Strategy* adds $450 million to its USD reserve, a *bitcoin* whale dormant for over seven years moves $188 million in BTC, and more.

Meanwhile, the *UK* published ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408046/uk-lays-out-tokenized-finance-roadmap-projected-33-billion-annual-boost?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) a *tokenized finance roadmap* with a projected £33 billion ($44 billion) annual boost.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🏛️ President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act while ethics fight looms*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/408054/trump-white-house-press-senate-advance-clarity-act-ethics-fight-looms?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss?utm_source=telegram1&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*President Trump* and *White House* officials launched a renewed push for the *Senate* to pass the *Clarity Act* , which would give the crypto industry its first federal regulatory framework.

* Congress returned from its July 4 recess with just a few weeks to act amid a limited calendar, and new bill text is expected this week ahead of a possible full Senate vote.
* Trump urged the Senate to pass the bill in tribute to *Sen. Lindsey Graham* , who died unexpectedly over the weekend, framing it as a race against China on crypto and AI.
* Graham was not a leading negotiator of crypto legislation, but he supported a few high-profile digital asset measures.
* White House crypto adviser *Patrick Witt* and *Sen. Cynthia Lummis* backed the push, with Witt saying Congress "cannot afford to delay any longer."
* A source said the current bill text does not include an ethics provision, a key sticking point negotiators have deb

 Inside Tech: Our interview with Arthur Mensch 


 Alex Hern

AI writer


 The Fable “blip”—three weeks during which Anthropic’s whizziest artificial-intelligence system was kept from users at the behest of America’s government—is likely to be remembered as an epochal moment in the history of AI. For Arthur Mensch, it was also a nice bit of advertising.

 The Economist

The Economist Today

Stephanie Studer

Senior digital editor



 As fears grow that a hostile America could one day cut off access to its globally dominant payment systems, countries from Brazil to Belgium are creating their own. That presents

a headache 
 for Visa and Mastercard.
